rules.name: Entrance Direction
rules.description: The main door should ideally face North, East, or North-East. These directions are considered auspicious and allow positive energy to enter the home.
rules.what_to_look_for: Identify the main entrance/front door of the apartment and determine its direction relative to the marked north. Look for doors on the perimeter walls of the floor plan.
rules.ideal: North, East, or North-East facing entrance
rules.avoid: South or South-West facing entrance, which Vastu considers inauspicious

rules.name: Kitchen Placement
rules.description: The kitchen should be located in the South-East corner of the home, which is governed by the fire element (Agni). This placement is believed to ensure health and prosperity.
rules.what_to_look_for: Identify the kitchen on the floor plan. Determine which quadrant or corner of the home it occupies relative to the marked north direction.
rules.avoid: North-East or South-West placement of the kitchen

rules.name: Master Bedroom Direction
rules.description: The master bedroom should be in the South-West part of the home. This direction is associated with stability, strength, and the earth element, promoting restful sleep.
rules.what_to_look_for: Identify the master bedroom (usually the largest bedroom) on the floor plan. Determine its position relative to the home's center and the marked north direction.
rules.ideal: South-West corner or area of the home
rules.avoid: North-East placement of the master bedroom, which Vastu reserves for prayer or meditation

rules.name: Bathroom Placement
rules.description: Bathrooms and toilets should avoid the North-East (Ishan corner) and the center of the home (Brahmasthan), as these are considered sacred energy zones.
rules.what_to_look_for: Identify all bathrooms and toilets on the floor plan. Check whether any fall in the North-East corner or near the center of the home.
rules.ideal: North-West or South-West placement for bathrooms
rules.avoid: North-East corner (Ishan) or the central zone of the home

rules.name: Brahmasthan (Center of Home)
rules.description: The Brahmasthan is the central zone of the home and should remain open, unobstructed, and free of heavy structures. It is considered the energy nucleus of the entire space.
rules.what_to_look_for: Identify the approximate center of the floor plan. Check whether it is open (e.g., a living area or open space) or blocked by walls, columns, toilets, or heavy furniture.
rules.ideal: Open living area, courtyard, or unobstructed space at the center
rules.avoid: Toilets, kitchens, pillars, or load-bearing walls at the center of the home
